nodes.label: Enterprise SaaS Agreement
nodes.content: ENTERPRISE SAAS MASTER SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT between Nimbus Analytics Pte. Ltd. and Harbor Retail Group Ltd., effective 15 March 2026.

nodes.label: 1. Commercial Terms
nodes.content: 1. Commercial Terms

nodes.label: 1.1 Access Scope
nodes.content: Provider grants Customer a non-exclusive right during the Subscription Term to access the production and staging environments identified in each Order Form for internal business operations.

nodes.label: 1.1.1 User Controls
nodes.content: Customer shall ensure Authorized Users use individual credentials, multi-factor authentication where available, and role-based access rights consistent with least privilege.

nodes.label: 1.2 Order Priority
nodes.content: Order Forms define metrics, subscription quantities, and pricing. Conflicting purchase order terms are rejected unless expressly accepted in writing by Provider.

nodes.label: 1.2.1 Change Controls
nodes.content: Scope expansion, additional modules, and seat increases require a signed change order or replacement Order Form and are billed pro rata unless otherwise stated.

nodes.label: 1.3 Invoicing Terms
nodes.content: Invoices are issued per Order Form schedule and payable within thirty (30) days. Taxes are exclusive unless expressly included.

nodes.label: 1.3.1 Fee Disputes
nodes.content: Customer must raise good-faith invoice disputes within ten (10) business days and pay undisputed amounts on time; parties shall escalate disputed amounts under commercial governance.

nodes.label: 2. Service Operations
nodes.content: 2. Service Operations

nodes.label: 2.1 SLA Measurement
nodes.content: Provider targets 99.9% monthly uptime excluding scheduled maintenance, emergency maintenance, force majeure, and third-party carrier outages outside Provider control.

nodes.label: 2.1.1 Credit Remedies
nodes.content: If monthly uptime falls below target, Customer may claim service credits according to the SLA schedule. Credits are Customer's sole monetary remedy for SLA breach.

nodes.label: 2.2 Incident Support
nodes.content: Provider shall provide support channels and response targets by severity level, including 24/7 support for Severity 1 production outages.

nodes.label: 2.2.1 Incident Governance
nodes.content: For major incidents, parties shall appoint incident managers, maintain a shared timeline, and perform post-incident review with corrective actions.

nodes.label: 2.3 Suspension Controls
nodes.content: Provider may suspend affected accounts for verified security abuse, legal violations, or sustained non-payment after notice where practicable.

nodes.label: 2.3.1 Limited Suspension
nodes.content: Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to limit suspension to impacted users, tenants, or API keys and preserve read-only export paths where safe.

nodes.label: 3. Data Security
nodes.content: 3. Data Security

nodes.label: 3.1 Data Processing
nodes.content: Provider processes Customer Data only to deliver and support services and in accordance with the Data Processing Addendum and applicable data protection law.

nodes.label: 3.1.1 Cross-border Transfers
nodes.content: Where cross-border transfers are required, parties shall implement valid transfer mechanisms, including standard contractual clauses where legally required.

nodes.label: 3.2 Security Audits
nodes.content: Provider maintains a risk-based security program including encryption, vulnerability management, access controls, logging, and annual external penetration tests.

nodes.label: 3.2.1 Audit Rights
nodes.content: Customer may request security attestations and summary audit artifacts no more than once annually, subject to confidentiality and third-party restrictions.

nodes.label: 3.3 Incident Response
nodes.content: Provider will notify Customer without undue delay and within seventy-two (72) hours after confirming a Security Incident impacting Customer Data.

nodes.label: 3.3.1 Remediation Duties
nodes.content: Provider will provide ongoing remediation updates, root cause analysis, and preventive action plans until closure, and cooperate with Customer's reasonable regulatory support requests.

nodes.label: 4. IP Liability
nodes.content: 4. IP Liability

nodes.label: 4.1 Output Ownership
nodes.content: Provider retains ownership of platform IP; Customer retains ownership of Customer Data and customer-specific outputs generated under the Subscription Services.

nodes.label: 4.1.1 Output Allocation
nodes.content: As between parties, Customer owns deliverable outputs generated from Customer prompts and data, while Provider retains rights in underlying models, orchestration logic, and generic improvements.

nodes.label: 4.2 Warranties Indemnity
nodes.content: Each party gives authority warranties; Provider gives conformity warranty to documentation; implied warranties are disclaimed to the maximum extent permitted by law.

nodes.label: 4.2.1 IP Remedies
nodes.content: For covered infringement claims, Provider may modify services, procure rights, or terminate affected services with pro-rated refund of prepaid unused fees.

nodes.label: 4.3 Liability Caps
nodes.content: Except excluded liabilities, each party's aggregate liability is capped at fees paid in the twelve (12) months before the event giving rise to the claim.

nodes.label: 4.3.1 Exclusions
nodes.content: Liability caps do not apply to fraud, willful misconduct, confidentiality breach, data protection obligations, or unpaid fees by Customer.

nodes.label: 4.4 Data Exit
nodes.content: Agreement term follows active Order Forms. Either party may terminate for uncured material breach; Customer receives a post-termination export window.

nodes.label: 4.4.1 Deletion Timeline
nodes.content: Customer may export Customer Data for sixty (60) days after termination; Provider deletes Customer Data from active systems within ninety (90) days unless retention is legally required.
